codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit adds the requested DistributedGroup and MLXDistributed API surface and the wrapper signatures line up with the MLX-C distributed headers, but the lifecycle requirement is not met because DistributedGroup.deinit is intentionally left empty, leaking every initialized or split group.

issues.description: DistributedGroup.deinit never releases the underlying mlx_distributed_group handle, so every group returned by mlx_distributed_init() or mlx_distributed_group_split() leaks the heap-allocated mlx::core::distributed::Group backing ctx. That misses the feature requirement for proper lifecycle handling with no leak/double-free.

DistributedGroup.deinit never releases the underlying mlx_distributed_group handle, so every group returned by mlx_distributed_init() or mlx_distributed_group_split() leaks the heap-allocated mlx::core::distributed::Group backing ctx. That misses the feature requirement for proper lifecycle handling with no leak/double-free.

sharedStateObservations.description: Mission guidance gives conflicting lifecycle expectations for distributed groups.
sharedStateObservations.observation: Clarify the mission guidance so it does not simultaneously require DistributedGroup to free in deinit and document that MLX-C exposes no public distributed-group free API. The current contradiction forced the worker to choose a leaking implementation to satisfy the rest of the feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/ronaldmannak/.factory/missions/019bac78-c826-4d65-837d-6a4194223ed4/AGENTS.md:29 says DistributedGroup frees in deinit, while /Users/ronaldmannak/Developer/Projects/Pico AI Homelab/mlx-swift/.factory/library/architecture.md:54 says mlx_distributed_group_free() is not publicly exposed in MLX-C v0.5.0; /Users/ronaldmannak/.factory/missions/019bac78-c826-4d65-837d-6a4194223ed4/worker-transcripts.jsonl:4 shows the worker spending time reconciling that mismatch and then accepting a leak.

sharedStateObservations.description: The swift-library-worker skill still mandates TDD for features whose tests are split into separate mission features.
sharedStateObservations.observation: Update the skill to note that tests-first can be skipped when the mission intentionally separates bindings work from later validation/test features; otherwise workers get contradictory process instructions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/ronaldmannak/Developer/Projects/Pico AI Homelab/mlx-swift/.factory/skills/swift-library-worker/SKILL.md:28-35 requires writing tests first, while /Users/ronaldmannak/.factory/missions/019bac78-c826-4d65-837d-6a4194223ed4/handoffs/2026-03-14T05-45-57-970Z__distributed-swift-bindings__b5bd2edd-2b76-4732-a9b0-1fd1a4d213f7.json:52-56 records the worker noting that this bindings-only feature was intentionally separated from the later distributed-single-process-tests feature.

summary: Fail. The wrapper surface largely matches the requested distributed API, but DistributedGroup.deinit does not free the underlying MLX-C/C++ group, so the feature misses the required no-leak lifecycle behavior.
